cases.prompt: Use the GitHub inspection tool to verify local GitHub access. Call git.run with command exactly: gh repo view --json nameWithOwner,url. Then reply with whether the command worked and the repo name.
List the scheduled tasks visible in workspace {{workspaceId}} using the scheduled task list tool. Then summarize how many tasks were returned.

Create a one-shot scheduled task in workspace {{workspaceId}} for agent {{agentId}}. The task should deliver this exact instruction: "Manual manager tool battery {{timestamp}}". Schedule it for {{futureIso}} UTC. Use scheduled_task.create, then reply with the created scheduled task id.
Read scheduled task {{scheduledTaskId}} in workspace {{workspaceId}} using scheduled_task.read. Then summarize its enabled flag and next_run_at.

Update scheduled task {{scheduledTaskId}} in workspace {{workspaceId}} using scheduled_task.update. Set enabled to false and add metadata {"manual_battery":"{{timestamp}}"}. Then reply with whether the update succeeded.
Disable scheduled task {{scheduledTaskId}} in workspace {{workspaceId}} using scheduled_task.delete. Then reply with whether it was disabled.
Snooze work item {{workItemId}} for 60 seconds using the snooze tool. Then reply with the new polling status.
Mark work item {{workItemId}} done using mark_done. Then reply with whether it was completed.

Dispatch a reviewer runner for work item {{workItemId}} using dispatch_runner. Use runner_kind "codex" and intent "review the latest local changes for correctness and missing tests". Then reply with the dispatch result.

Escalate work item {{workItemId}} to a human using escalate_to_human. Use trigger_kind "self_flagged", urgency "low", question "Manual tool battery escalation test - no action needed", and context_summary "Testing manager tool routing." Then reply with the escalation result.
